description.en:
A modular semantic framework for role-, attribute-, relationship-, and
         purpose-based access control over Digital Product Passport data,
         aligned with ESPR, GDPR, and ODRL 2.2.

definition.value: An activity is something that occurs over a period of time and acts upon or with entities; it may include consuming, processing, transforming, modifying, relocating, using, or generating entities.

An immutable, time-stamped record of the outcome of evaluating
         an AccessRequest against applicable AccessPolicies.
An entity is a physical, digital, conceptual, or other kind of thing with some fixed aspects; entities may be real or imaginary.

Environmental, temporal, geographic, or purpose-related
         metadata that conditions the evaluation of an access
         request.

A condition on a named attribute of the requesting Principal
         or target resource that must be satisfied for a policy's
         permissions to apply.

The declared purpose for which a Principal requests access,
         per GDPR Article 5(1)(b) purpose limitation.

Introduce a new data record into a DPP module where no prior
         record exists for that resource identifier.

Transfer a subset of one's own access rights to another
         Principal for a bounded scope and time period.

Remove a DPP resource record, subject to data retention
         obligations.
Modify the content of an existing DPP resource record.

Retrieve and inspect the content of a DPP resource without
         modifying it.
A natural person acting as a DPP data requester or owner.
An agent is something that bears some form of responsibility for an activity taking place, for the existence of an entity, or for another agent's activity.

The assurance level of the authentication mechanism used
         by the requesting Principal.
A jurisdictional constraint on where access may be exercised.

A named function or responsibility assigned to a Principal
         that determines which access policies apply.

An automated agent or software system acting as a DPP data
         consumer or producer.

A bounded, time-limited transfer of a subset of access rights
         from one Principal to another.
A time window within which access is permitted.

A formal, time-stamped expression of intent by a Principal
         to perform a specific Action on a specific DPP resource.

Any entity — human, organisation, or automated system — that
         may request access to DPP resources.

Evidence of a certification held by the requesting Principal
         that may unlock access to restricted DPP data.

A discrete, addressable unit of data within a Digital Product
         Passport that may be the target of an access request.

A declarative specification of permissions and prohibitions
         over DPP resources, scoped to a Role and optionally
         conditioned on contextual constraints.
A legal entity or organisation acting as a DPP stakeholder.
The number of sub-delegation hops permitted from this delegation.

scopeNote.value: Maximum allowed value is 1. Sub-delegation is prohibited by policy.

definition.value: The DPP resource(s) over which delegated rights are granted.
A textual description of the purpose for which data access is requested.
The ESPR-defined data category (DPP module) that contains this resource.

True if a deny-overrides conflict resolution strategy was
         invoked during this decision evaluation.
The jurisdiction in which this access constraint applies.

scopeNote.value: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, or the literals EU or EEA.

definition.value:
A purpose code that an access request must declare to activate
         this policy's permissions.

scopeNote.value:
Must be one of the purpose codes defined in PurposeContextShape:
         MAINTENANCE, COMPLIANCE_AUDIT, RECYCLING_PROCESSING,
         MARKET_SURVEILLANCE, CUSTOMS_INSPECTION, CONSUMER_INFORMATION,
         RESEARCH_DEVELOPMENT, SUPPLY_CHAIN_DUE_DILIGENCE.

definition.value: The CRUD action the Principal intends to perform.
The value against which the attribute is compared.
The Principal who grants delegated access rights.
True if the referenced certification is currently valid and in good standing.

True if this delegation is currently within its valid time window
         and has not been revoked.

Links an AccessPolicy to one or more AttributeConstraints that
         must all be satisfied for the policy's permissions to activate.
The UTC date-time at which this temporal access window closes.
The UTC date-time at which the delegation expires.
The name or identifier of the certification scheme.
The Principal responsible for the lifecycle of this policy.

The Principal who is the authoritative data owner of this
         DPP resource and may grant or revoke access.
The action(s) the delegatee is authorised to perform.
Links this AccessDecision to the AccessRequest it resolves.
The UTC date-time at which the access decision was recorded.
Assigns a single Access Control Role to a Principal.
Links an AccessRequest to the Principal who submitted it.
The comparison operator applied to the attribute value.

scopeNote.value: Allowed values: EQ, NEQ, GT, LT, GTE, LTE, IN.

definition.value: The Principal who receives delegated access rights.
Classification of the sensitivity level of this DPP resource.

scopeNote.value:
Allowed values: PUBLIC, RESTRICTED, CONFIDENTIAL,
         STRICTLY_CONFIDENTIAL.

definition.value: The result of evaluating the access request.

scopeNote.value: Allowed values: GRANT, DENY, DELEGATE, PENDING.

definition.value: The Role to which this AccessPolicy is addressed.

The AccessPolicy (or policies) that were evaluated to produce
         this decision.
The UTC date-time after which this policy expires.
The UTC date-time from which this policy is active.

Environmental or situational metadata that conditions the evaluation
         of this access request.
The eIDAS assurance level of the requester's authentication.
The name of the attribute being constrained.
The DPP resource the Principal intends to access.
The UTC date-time from which this temporal access window opens.
The UTC date-time at which the AccessRequest was submitted.
The UTC date-time from which the delegation is active.
